---

## Changes Made

### 1. `template/addons/next-auth/api-handler.ts` — GitHub provider (no Prisma)
Added `import GitHubProvider from "next-auth/providers/github"` and added it to the `providers` array alongside Discord, reading its credentials from `env.GITHUB_CLIENT_ID` / `env.GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET`. The existing `session` callback already handles the `user.id` injection and works for all providers identically.

### 2. `template/addons/next-auth/api-handler-prisma.ts` — GitHub provider (with Prisma adapter)
Same change as above. The `PrismaAdapter` is fully provider-agnostic — its `Account` model uses the generic `provider` / `providerAccountId` columns, so **no Prisma schema change is required**. The `auth-schema.prisma` already has the correct `Account`, `Session`, `User`, and `VerificationToken` models.

### 3. `template/addons/env/auth-schema.mjs` — Env var types (auth-only)
Added `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID: z.string()` and `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET: z.string()` to `serverSchema`. These are server-side only (no `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix), so they stay out of the client bundle.

### 4. `template/addons/env/auth-prisma-schema.mjs` — Env var types (auth + Prisma)
Same additions to the combined schema used when both NextAuth and Prisma are selected.

### 5. `template/base/.env-example` — Example env file
Added a `# Next Auth GitHub Provider` section with blank `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=` and `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=`, exactly matching the format of the existing Discord section.

### 6. `template/addons/next-auth/github-sign-in-button.tsx` *(new)*
A `GitHubSignInButton` React component that calls `signIn("github")` — passing the provider ID directly bypasses NextAuth's generic sign-in page and sends the user straight to GitHub OAuth. Includes an inline GitHub SVG logo and minimal inline styles (no Tailwind dependency, so it works in all template variants).

### 7. `template/page-studs/profile/with-auth.tsx` *(new)*
A protected profile page following the exact pattern of the existing `page-studs/index/` variants. Uses `useSession` (client-side, matching the existing auth showcase pattern) and redirects unauthenticated users to `/` via `useRouter`. Displays:
- `session.user.image` — the GitHub avatar URL (stored automatically by the GitHub provider)
- `session.user.name` — the GitHub display name (or login if no display name, since the GitHub provider maps `profile.name ?? profile.login`)
- `session.user.email` and `session.user.id` as supplementary info

### 8. `template/addons/trpc/profile-router.ts` *(new)*
A protected tRPC router using `createProtectedRouter()` — the exact same pattern as `protected-example-router.ts`. The `getProfile` query returns all GitHub session fields from `ctx.session.user`. This demonstrates that **the tRPC context already passes GitHub session data correctly** — `auth-context.ts` and `auth-prisma-context.ts` call `getServerSession` which populates the session regardless of which provider was used to authenticate.
